content.problemStatement:
You are given two positive integers NNN and KKK, where K≤NK \le NK≤N. Find a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ such that:


for each valid iii, AiA_iAi​ is either iii or −i-i−i
there are exactly KKK values of iii such that 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N and A1+A2+…+Ai>0A_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_i \gt 0A1​+A2​+…+Ai​>0
If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one of them. It can be proved that at least one solution always exists.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
